decisions.title: Plugins in `.claude/skills` auto-load (no marketplace) — could eliminate the activation gap
`/reload-skills` — lighter alternative to `/reload-plugins` for skills-only updates
`/fewer-permission-prompts` bundled skill — auto-generate allowed-tools allowlist
decisions.note: Added a 'Reducing permission prompts' subsection to workflow.md Tips & Best Practices recommending the built-in /fewer-permission-prompts as first diagnostic, with the fix-allowed-tools-for-the-long-term caveat.

decisions.title: Enforce /bx:health read-only invariant via `disallowed-tools` frontmatter (v2.1.152)
decisions.note: User-directed registration after first dogfood run: per-skill opportunity surfaced outside the pain-point gate. Proposed: add `disallowed-tools: Edit, Write, Task` to bx/skills/health/SKILL.md frontmatter.

decisions.title: Declare `stitch-skills` as a plugin dependency in bx plugin.json (v2.1.143 dependency enforcement)
decisions.note: User-directed registration. CAVEAT verified against release notes: v2.1.143 enforcement is enable/disable-time only (`plugin disable` refuses when depended-on; `plugin enable` force-enables transitive deps) — install-time resolution NOT confirmed; check code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference before applying.

decisions.title: Stop-hook `additionalContext` could automate the end-of-session /bx:save reminder (v2.1.163)
decisions.note: User-directed registration. Stop/SubagentStop hooks can return hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext without being labeled a hook error — a Stop hook in bx/hooks/hooks.json could inject the '/bx:save before you go' reminder that workflow.md currently leaves to convention.

decisions.title: `Skill(name *)` wildcard prefix-match fixed (v2.1.139) — unblocks the Skill(bx-*)→Skill(bx:*) settings migration
decisions.note: User-directed registration. NOTE: changelog lane's digest also attributed this fix to v2.1.145 — verified against release bodies, it ships in v2.1.139 only. Also: .claude/settings.local.json is absent in this clone, so the migration may be moot — verify ~/.claude/settings.json on this machine before applying.

decisions.title: SessionStart `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` — persist env vars to all Bash calls (addresses no-env-var-persistence)
decisions.note: Added CLAUDE_ENV_FILE blocks to session-start-context.sh + .ps1 persisting PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 + PYTHONUTF8=1 session-wide (guarded no-op when unset); updated both scripts' read-only design-rule comments. Smoke-check next session before retiring per-call UTF-8 prefixes in /bx:seo.

decisions.title: Task tool renamed to Agent (v2.1.63, alias retained) — bx frontmatter and prose still say Task
decisions.note: Renamed bare `Task` to `Agent` in allowed-tools of 7 SKILL.md files (arch/clean/evolve/save/seo/tests/webdesign — webdesign + save/references/mode-update.md were orchestrator completeness catches beyond the lane's list) and updated tool-name prose in 5 skill bodies + README.md. TaskCreate/TaskList/TaskGet/TaskUpdate (task-tracker tools) deliberately untouched.

decisions.title: Claude Design (Anthropic-native design engine) — candidate to replace Google Stitch in /bx:webdesign once it ships an API/MCP
decisions.note: User-directed registration after this session's research (finding is NOT from the changelog/docs/community lanes — Claude Design is a separate Anthropic Labs product, launched 2026-04-17, Opus-powered). Strong long-term fit for the /bx:webdesign design engine: same vendor as the harness, codebase-aware design-system extraction (maps onto Phase 1), exports standalone HTML. BLOCKED today: Claude Design is interactive web-only with NO API/MCP/CLI, so the headless MCP-driven skill (generate_screen_from_text / get_screen / edit_screens, screen IDs persisted in state.json) has nothing to call — a swap is impossible now, not just inadvisable. Trigger: re-evaluate when Anthropic ships a Claude Design API/MCP or makes the 'Handoff to Claude Code' bundle scriptable; source_content_hash tracks the announcement's 'integrations coming weeks' line, so a change there re-raises this. Related v2 question worth a brainstorm: whether Opus-native restyle generation removes the need for ANY external design engine (Stitch or Claude Design) at all.

decisions.title: Auto-mode now blocks destructive git (`git clean -fd`, `restore`, `reset --hard`) — /bx:webdesign Phase-3 rollback may be intercepted (v2.1.183)
decisions.note: Lane finding, certainty 0.55 — NOT yet verified against a live run. /bx:webdesign Phase 3's documented per-page-failure rollback (`git restore .` + `git clean -fd`) and the git-safety invariant may be auto-blocked by the new classifier unless the session reads as an explicit request to discard local work. VERIFY during the pending webdesign dogfood before treating as confirmed breakage; if intercepted, make the cleanup legible as agent-initiated or add a user-confirmation step before Phase 3 first runs. Also relevant: new `autoMode.classifyAllShell` setting (v2.1.193).

decisions.title: Skills-directory plugins (`<name>@skills-dir`) auto-load with no marketplace/cache — could close the activation gap (docs + v2.1.178)
decisions.note: STRONGEST evidence yet for Next-Steps #1. A folder with `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` under `~/.claude/skills/` loads as `<name>@skills-dir` next session, discovered in place (not copied to the plugin cache) — personal-scope loads in every project with no install/update step, closing the plugin-cache-staleness/activation gap outright. Caveats: SKILL.md edits apply live but hooks/.mcp.json/agents/output-styles changes still need /reload-plugins; project-scope @skills-dir plugins don't walk up to repo root (launch from root). Cross-lane consolidated: docs plugins-reference (primary) corroborated by changelog v2.1.178 (nested .claude/skills contextual auto-load). Distinct-source companion to open findings 19758d30 (v2.1.157) + 0e4083ea (/reload-skills) under this same pain slug. Evaluate converting bx from marketplace→skills-dir; if adopted, update the S37 'Plugin approach' decision + README install steps. ADDENDUM 2026-07-22: the docs lane re-emitted this same finding_id from a DIFFERENT section of the same page — 'Version management', which states '/plugin update or auto-update fires' and confirms bx's omitted-`version` field means commit-SHA versioning ('users get updates on every new commit'). Implies an automatic update path may already exist alongside the manual step; the fetched section does not specify the auto-update trigger cadence or scope, so verify live behavior before softening the manual `/plugin update bx` + `/reload-plugins` instructions in README.md / workflow.md / fix-mode-evolve.md. stored source_content_hash intentionally left anchored to the original skills-dir excerpt (open entries have no hash-trigger semantics; Rules 3/5 cover rejected/deferred only).

decisions.title: `claude mcp login/logout` CLI (v2.1.186) — documents the Stitch MCP auth step without the interactive /mcp menu
decisions.note: Useful for the still-pending one-time Stitch MCP install/auth (Next Steps #2). setup-stitch-mcp.md currently documents only `claude mcp add ... stitch ...`; add `claude mcp login stitch` as the documented auth step (with `--no-browser` for SSH/headless) and cross-reference from webdesign/SKILL.md's registration note. Low-effort doc addition, good --fix candidate.

decisions.title: `Tool(param:value)` permission syntax e.g. `Agent(model:opus)` (v2.1.178) — permission-layer guard against the S43 Opus-subagent bug
decisions.note: APPLIED 2026-07-05: added a 'Guarding subagent model routing: Agent(model:…) deny rules' subsection to workflow.md Tips & Best Practices (after the /fewer-permission-prompts entry) recommending an `Agent(model:opus)`/`Agent(model:haiku)` deny rule in .claude/settings.json as a permission-layer backstop atop the frontmatter model: sonnet declarations (guards the S43 Opus-subagent class of bug). Note: settings.local.json is absent in this clone (see finding 79708ccf) — the tip is advisory guidance, not wired up here.

decisions.title: `user-invocable: false` is inert on plugin agents — remove from all 18 bx/agents/*.md (docs, cert 0.80)
decisions.note: APPLIED 2026-07-05: removed the inert `user-invocable: false` line from all 18 bx/agents/*.md via a uniform perl sweep (`perl -i -ne 'print unless /^user-invocable: false$/'`); verified 0 remaining + all 18 frontmatter blocks intact. Docs confirm `user-invocable` is a SKILL.md-only field, inert on plugin subagents (dispatched via Agent+subagent_type, never the / menu). SKILL.md usage deliberately untouched.

decisions.title: v2.1.181 fixes settings ENOENT under symlinked ~/.claude — reduces urgency of S37 symlink retirement (informational)
decisions.note: INFORMATIONAL — no action. Makes the OLD symlinked ~/.claude setup (README migration <details>) more crash-resistant on machines that haven't retired symlinks; does not advance or substitute for the planned retirement. Recorded so it isn't re-announced as new next run; reject in a --fix pass to clear.

decisions.title: v2.1.176 fixes Linux sandbox start under absolute-target symlinked settings — reduces urgency of S37 symlink retirement (informational)
decisions.note: INFORMATIONAL — no action, same class as finding b866308e (different bug: absolute-target symlink on Linux sandbox vs. relative-target ENOENT). Reduces breakage risk for machines still on the old symlinked setup on Linux; does not change the retirement plan. Recorded to prevent re-announcement; reject in --fix to clear.

decisions.title: Skills/commands edited mid-session now appear in the slash menu without a restart (v2.1.216) — may soften the hard /reload-plugins requirement
decisions.note: APPLIED 2026-07-22 as a HEDGED doc note, not a behavioural claim — v2.1.216's fix covers in-place edits to already-loaded skill/command files, which is a different mechanism from a plugin-cache refresh pulling a new marketplace commit; that distinction is unverified, so both edits keep the manual steps fully intact (no-remove-manual-steps convention). README.md 'Updating to the latest version': appended a sentence noting the 2.1.216 change and why `/plugin marketplace update` + `/plugin update bx` are still how you pick up someone else's commits. fix-mode-evolve.md 'Next steps': appended a parenthetical to the `/reload-plugins` bullet saying the same, ending 'keep running the refresh until someone confirms otherwise'. SCOPE CORRECTION: the lane listed workflow.md in affected_files, but its /plugin-update hits (lines 59, 87) are inside the `cc` launcher-script comments — launch-time behaviour, unrelated to mid-session refresh — so workflow.md was deliberately excluded. This is a scope fix, not a partial application under the S45 sibling-echo rule. Fourth distinct source under this pain slug (companions: 19758d30 v2.1.157, 0e4083ea /reload-skills, 15742589 plugins-reference), all still open.

decisions.title: v2.1.216 fixes Bash permission checking for compound statements with redirects inside `&&` lists — informational, covers patterns bx already uses
decisions.note: REJECTED 2026-07-22 in the --fix pass — nothing to act on, which is the intended terminal state for an informational finding, not a disagreement with it. Upstream fixed a permission-classifier misjudgment in bx's favour: bx/bin/gsc-parse-helper (`command -v "$cand" >/dev/null 2>&1 && "$cand" -c '...'`) and bx/skills/seo/references/gsc-cache.md (`[ "$NO_CACHE" != "1" ] && [ -n "$(find ... 2>/dev/null)" ]`) use exactly the fixed compound-redirect shape. Rejected to clear it from the carried-forward list; per lifecycle Rule 3 it re-raises only if a future run re-emits it with a CHANGED source_content_hash. NOTE the tension with still-open finding 093df977 (v2.1.214) which pushes the opposite direction — fail-CLOSED on FD-redirect forms — and remains unverified; do not read this rejection as 'bx's redirect usage is confirmed safe'.

decisions.title: v2.1.214 Bash permission checks now fail CLOSED on file-descriptor redirect forms — may cause new prompts on bx's pervasive `2>/dev/null` usage
decisions.note: Certainty 0.50 — NOT verified as a live regression. `2>/dev/null` / `2>&1` appears across bx/scripts/session-start-context.sh, bx/skills/health/SKILL.md (`!`-injected commands), bx/skills/resume/SKILL.md, bx/skills/seo/SKILL.md and bx/skills/seo/references/gsc-cache.md. Fail-closed means previously auto-approved forms now prompt. ACTION: smoke-check next session (run a few of these, watch for new prompts) before touching allowed-tools. Note the opposite direction from finding e3964f2f (v2.1.216 relaxes a different misclassification).

decisions.title: `${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}` IS a real substitution per current docs — the S39-era 'not a real variable' comments in bx/bin/gsc-parse-helper + seo/SKILL.md are now factually stale
decisions.note: APPLIED 2026-07-22 — corrected the stale claim in both files without weakening the S39 lesson. The precise correction: ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR} IS real, but Claude Code expands it only as text pre-processing of a skill's rendered markdown and of Bash rules in allowed-tools (v2.1.129+); it is NOT a shell variable, so anything reaching the shell with it unexpanded still gets an empty string — which is what the S33 bug actually was. bx/bin/gsc-parse-helper: rewrote the 'Why this wrapper exists' header comment. bx/skills/seo/SKILL.md ~line 903: rewrote the trailing parenthetical. DELIBERATELY PRESERVED: the ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} half of both warnings — that claim is still accurate (it is not exported to the Bash tool environment; the hooks.json usage is a different context). NOT DONE (deliberately deferred): collapsing the bin/ PATH-launcher indirection to `allowed-tools: Bash(${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/references/gsc-parse-helper.py *)`. The launcher is verified working since S39 and /bx:seo has still never had a real run — do not touch the dispatch path before that run.

decisions.title: Docs document only `Agent(<subagent-name>)` deny rules — the `Agent(model:opus)` tip applied 2026-07-05 is uncorroborated by the sub-agents/settings pages
decisions.note: APPLIED 2026-07-22, but the lane's PREMISE WAS WRONG — resolved by orchestrator verification against https://code.claude.com/docs/en/permissions#match-by-input-parameter (outside the docs lane's pinned allowlist, which is why the lane missed it). `Agent(model:opus)` IS real and documented under 'Match by input parameter'; the v2.1.178-sourced tip applied in finding 2d6b0e5f was NOT wrong about the syntax. The finding survived for a different and more serious reason: the same docs section states 'a parameter the model omits is never matched', and the S43 bug was a GENERIC subagent dispatched with no `model` param at all — so the deny rule would never have fired on the exact bug the tip claimed to guard. Rewrote workflow.md's 'Guarding subagent model routing' section: 'permission-layer backstop' → 'partial permission-layer backstop', and replaced the 'Belt-and-suspenders … stops a regression from ever spawning a non-Sonnet bx agent' overclaim with the omitted-parameter limit + two further caveats (literal-input comparison before normalization, so alias `opus` matches but a full model ID does not; parameter rules valid in deny/ask only, never allow) and a pointer back to frontmatter `model:` + dispatch-by-name as the durable guard. LESSON for scan-docs.md: the pinned allowlist lacks the permissions page, which owns permission-rule syntax — consider adding it. Orchestrator normalized the lane's `bx:*/agent-model-deny-rule-syntax` to `bx:agents/...` per the bx:<skill>/<area> capability-string form.
